source_jobs.title: Investment Banking Analyst
source_jobs.description:
Our Firm

Agentis Capital Advisors is a leading global financial advisor with a reputation for delivering unparalleled value and advice to its clients. The firm has been the recipient of numerous industry rewards in recent years, including 2024 P3 Awards Financial Advisor of the Year, multiple 2023 industry deal awards, Proximo Infra's 2022 North American Financial Advisor of the Year, and IJGlobal's 2022 Judge's Choice Award. Our guiding principles and ability to generate value for clients sets us apart. Agentis acts as a sell-side and buy-side advisor on a wide variety of global transactions including renewable power, energy, digital, transportation, and public-private partnerships. Our clients include leading infrastructure funds, pension funds, contractors, and governments.

Agentis Capital Advisors is a business segment of Agentis Capital Partners, which operates a synergistic platform across four main business segments: Agentis Capital Partners (principal investments), Agentis Capital Advisors (financial advisory and asset management services), Agentis Capital Mining Partners (mining advisory), and Agentis Capital Markets (capital markets).


The Opportunity

Our analyst program is designed to cultivate the quantitative and qualitative skills necessary to excel in an investment banking career and as a future executive. You will receive both formal and on-the-job training throughout your tenure. You will gain increasing levels of responsibility as you progress in your career, from building financial models, to managing multiple deal workstreams, to leading client relationships. You will be given unparalleled exposure and opportunities, including the chance to work on numerous transactions at the start of your career, with the only constraint on your role being your own capacity, rather than your rank level. As a result, our team members achieve accelerated career growth. You will be recognized and rewarded for your individual performance within a close-knit team and meritocratic culture. The only constraint on your role, responsibilities, and compensation will be your own performance.

This is a 1-year contract position with the potential for renewal, based on performance. Compensation will include a competitive base salary in USD and a performance-based bonus.


Your Contribution

As an integral member of the advisory team, you will be challenged to accept increasing levels of responsibility as you develop your skills and reputation. Your work will directly impact our clients and the firm.

Your role will include:

 - Participating in the execution of equity and debt offerings, mergers and acquisitions, public-private partnerships, and principal investments

 - Developing complex financial models for infrastructure transactions, including the use of macros and VBA programming

 - Assisting in the management of due diligence processes, including managing third-party advisors

 - Developing asset valuations using a variety of approaches

 - Preparing client presentations and actively participating in discussions

 - Evaluating investment opportunities and management of existing investments

 - Researching potential investment banking opportunities; providing research on target companies, sectors, industries, and geographies

 - Maintaining marketing materials for the firm, including creating pitch decks to be presented to potential clients

As a member of a high-performing team working in a fast-paced environment, there will be times where you will be required to work beyond a normal workday or workweek to fulfill the responsibilities of the role.


Our Differentiators

Breadth of experience and level of responsibility

 - Exceptional level of responsibility on complex transactions

 - Wide breadth of work across verticals, financial products and principal opportunities

 - Opportunities to work on large global transactions

 - Secondment opportunities within Agentis portfolio companies

Training

 - Continuous internal tr

source_jobs.title: Vice President, Investment Banking - Infrastructure M&A Advisory
Investment Banking Associate - Public Private Partnership Transaction Advisory
source_jobs.description:
Our Firm

Agentis Capital Advisors is a leading global financial advisor with a reputation for delivering unparalleled value and advice to its clients. The firm has been the recipient of numerous recent industry rewards, including Financial Advisor of the Year in North America by P3 Awards (2024), Proximo (2022), and IJGlobal (2021).

The Opportunity

Our Associate program is designed to cultivate the quantitative and qualitative skills necessary to excel in an investment banking career. You will receive both formal and on-the-job training throughout your tenure. You will gain increasing levels of responsibility as you progress in your career, from overseeing the execution of financial model-related tasks, to managing multiple deal workstreams, to leading client relationships. You will be given unparalleled exposure and opportunities, including the chance to lead numerous transactions. As a result, our team members often achieve accelerated career growth and earn industry recognition. You will be recognized and rewarded for your individual performance within a close-knit team and meritocratic culture.



Your Contribution

As an integral member of the global public-private partnership (P3) advisory team, you will be challenged to accept increasing levels of responsibility as you develop your skills and reputation. Your work will directly impact our clients and the firm.

Your role will include:

 - Participating in the execution of PPP transactions

 - Developing complex financial models, including the use of macros and VBA programming

 - Assisting in the management of due diligence processes, including managing third-party advisors

 - Preparing client presentations and actively participating in discussions

 - Researching potential business development opportunities

 - Maintaining marketing materials for the firm, including creating pitches to be presented to potential clients

As a member of a high-performing team working in a fast-paced environment, there will be times where you will be required to work beyond a normal workday or workweek to fulfill the responsibilities of the role.



Our Differentiators

Breadth of experience and level of responsibility

 - Exceptional level of responsibility on complex transactions, allowing for rapid career progression

 - Opportunities to work on large global transactions

Training

 - Continuous internal training program

 - Internal leadership development program

Culture and approach

 - Entrepreneurial culture with a flat organization structure

 - Direct exposure to Partners and senior management across Agentis' offices

 - Defined path to achieving Partner, with all individuals entering the organization receiving the opportunity to become a full Partner in the business

Compensation and employee ownership

 - Uncapped performance-based bonus

 - Employee ownership program that commences for all individuals from the end of their first full fiscal-year of employment

Other benefits

 - Paid sabbatical every two years of employment

 - Comprehensive benefits including extended health care
   
   

Your Skills and Experience

 - A minimum of 2-3 years of experience in PPP transactions, including financial modeling

 - Ability to concisely communicate complex concepts and ideas verbally and in writing

 - Ability to take initiative and work independently with minimal supervision

 - Desire to assume increasing levels of responsibility and seek solutions to complex problems

 - Committed to producing accurate and thorough work product under tight deadlines

 - Ability to learn quickly and adapt in a fast-paced work environment

 - Resilient mindset in the face of adversity

 - Ability to work effectively in a team environment
   
   

Position Type

 - Permanent Full-Time

 - Desired start date: Immediately

 - Location: Toronto or Vancouver

Relocation required: This role is based in Canada; candidates must be willing and able to relocate to Canada

source_jobs.title: Managing Director - Digital Infrastructure, Investment Banking
source_jobs.description:
Our Firm

Agentis Capital Advisors is a leading global financial advisor with a reputation for delivering unparalleled value and strategic advice to its clients. Agentis acts as a sell-side and buy-side advisor with a focus on global transactions involving infrastructure, renewable power, energy, digital, transportation, and public-private partnerships.

With more than $125 billion in evaluated transactions and multiple industry awards, including North American Financial Advisor of the Year, we apply disciplined thinking, practical innovation, and an ownership mindset to every mandate. Our guiding principles and ability to generate value are our differentiators, which have earned the trust, respect, and business from leading infrastructure funds, pension funds, contractors, and governments.


Our Differentiators

 - Direct responsibility on complex, high-profile transactions across verticals and financial products

 - Direct involvement in SPAC and de-SPAC initiatives, with the opportunity to participate in associated economics

 - Competitive compensation with meaningful employee ownership and full benefits

 - Structured training and leadership development, including direct mentorship from Partners

 - Paid sabbatical every two years of employment

 - Entrepreneurial culture with a defined and achievable path to partnership

 - Co-investment rights alongside Partners on select principal opportunities
   

The Opportunity

Due to growth and an expanding remit across public-market acquisition vehicles, Agentis Capital Advisors is seeking a Managing Director, Investment Banking to lead and grow its digital infrastructure practice.

The Managing Director will play a pivotal role as part of the leadership team for Agentis’ North American investment banking practice, splitting time between (i) originating financial advisory mandates across digital infrastructure (and adjacent verticals), and (ii) leading and supporting SPAC and de-SPAC origination and execution across publicly listed acquisition vehicles. As a subject matter expert in digital infrastructure (including data centers, fiber, and towers), the Managing Director will also provide deal execution leadership and mentorship to junior bankers across the team.

The ideal candidate is an accomplished M&A banker with deep digital infrastructure sector expertise, a track record of sourcing and winning mandates, and a reputation for outstanding client service. An established network of existing or prospective clients in North America is expected.


Key Responsibilities

Deal Origination & Revenue Generation:

 - Identify and pursue new M&A advisory opportunities in digital infrastructure (such as data centers, fiber, and towers) and adjacent energy verticals, aligned with Agentis’ platform and growth strategy

 - Lead and support SPAC target origination and de-SPAC execution, screening private and public companies against SPAC-over-alternatives criteria

 - Lead pitch presentations to win mandates with the support of a talented, experienced team

 - Meet individual and firm revenue targets through successful deal closures, including SPAC and de-SPAC mandates

Senior Client Relationship Management:

 - Serve as the primary point of contact for investors, C-suite executives, board members, and government officials.

 - Build long-term partnerships with institutional investors such as pension funds, private wealth offices, and infrastructure private equity firms.

Strategic Advisory:

 - Provide senior counsel on M&A, capital raising, and complex financing structures across digital infrastructure and adjacent verticals

 - Originate and support financial advisory mandates as required, drawing on sector expertise and target-screening capability across private and public-market opportunities

 - Advise clients on the impact of market dynamics, industry trends, and regulatory developments

Leadership & Team Mentorship:

 - Lead, motivate, and mentor deal teams to meet execution deliver

source_jobs.title: Analyst, Asset Management Services
source_jobs.description:
Our Firm

Agentis Capital Advisors is a leading global financial advisor with a reputation for delivering unparalleled value and advice to its clients. The firm has been the recipient of numerous industry rewards in recent years, including 2024 P3 Awards Financial Advisor of the Year, multiple 2023 industry deal awards, Proximo Infra's 2022 North American Financial Advisor of the Year, and IJGlobal's 2022 Judge's Choice Award. Our guiding principles and ability to generate value for clients sets us apart. Agentis acts as a sell-side and buy-side advisor on a wide variety of global transactions including renewable power, energy, digital, transportation, and public-private partnerships. Our clients include leading infrastructure funds, pension funds, contractors, and governments.

Agentis Capital Advisors is a business segment of Agentis Capital Partners, which operates a synergistic platform across four main business segments: Agentis Capital Partners (principal investments), Agentis Capital Advisors (financial advisory and asset management services), Agentis Capital Mining Partners (mining advisory), and Agentis Capital Markets (capital markets).

The Opportunity

As an integral member of the Asset Management Services team, you will be challenged to accept increasing levels of responsibility as you develop your skills and reputation. You will work closely with senior staff and have exposure to a wide variety of asset types and valuation methodologies. Your work will directly impact our clients and the firm.

Your role will include:

 - Building, maintaining, and managing complex financial models for infrastructure transactions, with a focus on incorporating financial and operational updates

 - Developing asset and portfolio/fund valuations using a variety of approaches

 - Preparing client presentations and memos and participating actively in associated client discussions

 - Actively participating and supporting business development opportunities

 - Representing Agentis Capital in Vancouver’s finance community by attending industry and networking events

Our Differentiators

Breadth of experience and level of responsibility

 - Opportunities for accelerated career growth with a highly respected and rapidly growing firm

 - Exceptional level of responsibility on complex portfolios

 - Wide breadth of work across verticals

 - Opportunities to work alongside the infrastructure team on advisory transactions

Training

 - Continuous internal training program

 - Internal leadership development program

Culture and approach

 - Entrepreneurial culture with a flat organization structure

 - Defined path to achieving Partner, with all individuals entering the organization receiving the opportunity to become a full Partner in the business

Compensation and employee ownership

 - Above-market base salary versus Canadian competitors

 - Performance-based bonus

 - Employee ownership program that commences for all individuals from the end of their first full fiscal-year of employment

Other benefits

 - Immediate access to principal investment opportunities alongside Partners

 - Paid sabbatical every two years of employment

 - Comprehensive benefits including extended health care

 - Agentis will cover all necessary expenses associated with acquiring CFA or CPA certifications including: exam fees up to one attempt per module, all other module fees, CFE fees, and annual dues

Your Skills and Experience

 - 0 - 3 years of industry experience (strong accounting knowledge preferred)

 - An undergraduate degree in finance, business, accounting, engineering, economics, statistics

 - Highly motivated with a combination of strong financial/analytical skills, excellent communication abilities and an entrepreneurial disposition

 - Desire to assume increasing levels of authority and seek solutions to complex problems

 - Committed to producing meticulous, accurate, and thorough work product under tight deadlines

 - Exceptional level of dedi

source_jobs.title: Director, Investment Banking - Renewables
Director, Investment Banking - Infrastructure M&A Advisory
Vice President, Investment Banking - Public Private Partnership Transaction Advisory
source_jobs.description:
Our Firm

Agentis Capital Advisors is a leading global financial advisor with a reputation for delivering unparalleled value and advice to its clients. The firm has been the recipient of numerous recent industry rewards, including Financial Advisor of the Year in North America by P3 Awards (2024), Proximo (2022), and IJGlobal (2021).

The Opportunity

As Vice President, you will play a pivotal leadership role in leading our North American financial advisory practice. You will be expected to pitch financial advisory mandates, manage complex transactions concurrently, build and train a growing team of practitioners, and consistently provide high-quality, bespoke services to our clients. This role requires a strategic mindset, strong leadership skills, a deep understanding of the P3 finance landscape, and expertise in deal management and technical delivery.

Your Contribution

As an integral member of the global public-private partnership (P3) advisory team, you will be challenged to accept increasing levels of responsibility as you develop your skills and reputation. Your work will directly impact our clients and the firm.

Your role will include:

 - Lead transaction teams to successfully execute PPP mandates

 - Lead and support the structuring, and execution of debt and equity financings, including engaging with lenders and investors through to financial close

 - Developing complex financial models, including the use of macros and VBA programming

 - Assisting in the management of due diligence processes, including managing third-party advisors

 - Mentor, develop, and manage junior team members

 - Preparing client presentations and actively participating in discussions

 - Researching potential business development opportunities

Our Differentiators

 - Exceptional level of responsibility on complex transactions across verticals and financial products;

 - Opportunities to work on global transactions;

 - Continuous internal training and leadership development programs;

 - Competitive compensation, employee ownership, and comprehensive benefits package;

 - Paid sabbatical every two years of employment;

 - Entrepreneurial culture with a clear path to partnership; and

 - Access to principal investment opportunities alongside Partners.

Your Skills and Experience

 - A minimum of 5-6 years of experience in PPP transactions, including financial modeling

 - Strong deal management and technical delivery capabilities

 - Ability to concisely communicate complex concepts and ideas verbally and in writing

 - Strong business development and client relationship skills

 - Demonstrated ability to structure and execute PPP mandates

 - Committed to producing accurate and thorough work product under tight deadlines

 - Ability to learn quickly and adapt in a fast-paced work environment

 - Ability to work with geographically dispersed teams

Position Type

 - Permanent Full-Time

 - Desired start date: Immediately

 - Location: Toronto or Vancouver

Relocation required: This role is based in Canada; candidates must be willing and able to relocate to Canada

source_jobs.title: Director, Investment Banking - Infrastructure M&A Advisory
Investment Banking Winter Analyst - Infrastructure M&A Advisory - Winter 2027
source_jobs.description:
Our Firm

Agentis Capital Advisors is a leading global financial advisor with a reputation for delivering unparalleled value and advice to its clients. The firm has been the recipient of numerous industry rewards in recent years, including 2024 P3 Awards Financial Advisor of the Year. Our guiding principles and ability to generate value for clients sets us apart. Agentis acts as a sell-side and buy-side advisor on a wide variety of global transactions including renewable power, energy, digital, transportation, and public-private partnerships. Our clients include leading infrastructure funds, pension funds, contractors, and governments.

Agentis Capital Advisors is a business segment of Agentis Capital Partners, which operates a synergistic platform across four main business segments: Agentis Capital Partners (principal investments), Agentis Capital Advisors (financial advisory and asset management services), Agentis Capital Mining Partners (mining advisory), and Agentis Capital Markets (capital markets).

The Opportunity

The intern program includes a formal program of instruction in project finance, financial statement analysis, and financial modeling. Analyst interns are expected to demonstrate financial modeling capabilities through real-world applications and will be invited to work on active projects. You will be recognized and rewarded for your individual performance within a close-knit team and meritocratic culture. Upon successful completion of the internship, interns have the opportunity to be invited back to join Agentis in a full-time position.

Your Contribution

As an analyst intern, you can expect to be challenged from day one by complex, high-pressure assignments that will directly impact our clients and the firm. As a member of our advisory team, you will work closely in groups led by senior partners and will have exposure to a wide variety of transactions within a performance-based culture.

Your role will include:

 - Participating in the execution of equity and debt offerings, mergers and acquisitions, public-private partnerships, and principal investments

 - Assisting in the development of complex financial models for infrastructure transactions, including the use of macros and VBA programming

 - Assisting in the management of due diligence processes, including managing third-party advisors

 - Developing asset valuations using a variety of approaches

 - Preparing client presentations and participating in discussions

 - Assisting the senior team in the evaluation of investment opportunities and management of existing investments

 - Researching potential investment banking opportunities; providing research on target companies, sectors, industries, and geographies

 - Maintaining marketing materials for the firm, including creating pitch decks to be presented to potential clients.

 - As a member of a high-performing team working in a fast-paced environment, there will be times where you will be required to work beyond a normal workday or workweek to fulfill the responsibilities of the role.

Our Differentiators

 - Competitive compensation packages

 - Direct exposure to senior team members and industry leaders

 - Incredible opportunities for career growth including analyst track

 - Opportunities to work on projects with one of Agentis’ portfolio companies in other sectors including consulting, healthcare, fintech, and agriculture

 - Participation in Agentis’ internal training modules including a leadership development program

 - Regular team events

 - Potential to be invited back for a full-time position

Your Skills and Experience

 - An undergraduate degree in accounting, finance, commerce, business, statistics, computer science, engineering, mathematics, or other related areas

 - Graduating by Summer/Fall 2027

 - Exceptional academic results (minimum cGPA of 80% or 3.7/4.0)

 - Ability to concisely communicate complex concepts and ideas verbally and in writing

 - Ability to take initiative a

source_jobs.title: Principal Investments Fall Analyst - Fall 2026
source_jobs.description:
Our Firm

Agentis Capital Partners ("ACP") operates as the family office of the founding partners of Agentis, a diversified platform with businesses spanning principal investments, financial advisory, capital markets, and asset management services. Since its founding in 2009, the Agentis platform has advised on more than $30bn of transactions across infrastructure, mining, and energy, while ACP has built and managed a long-standing portfolio of principal investments across technology, industrial, and mixed industrial-technology businesses.

ACP actively pursues both active and passive principal investments and currently manages a portfolio of more than 20 businesses. The firm has consistently made principal investments over the past decade, recycling capital into high-value opportunities and working closely with management teams and boards to create long-term value.

The Opportunity

The Principal Investments Internship provides a unique opportunity to work with one of Agentis’ partner firms, alongside its principal investments team and in collaboration with the founding partners of Agentis. Please note that this is not a direct position with Agentis Capital Advisors; the internship will be with ACP or another Agentis affiliate/partner organization.

Unlike a traditional infrastructure investment banking internship, this role focuses on evaluating, executing, and managing principal investments, including direct equity investments and co-investments alongside advisory clients. Interns will gain exposure to the full lifecycle of principal investing - from sourcing and evaluating opportunities to supporting due diligence, transaction execution, and portfolio company strategy. The role offers meaningful responsibility, hands-on experience, and direct exposure to senior leadership across a wide range of industries.

Strong-performing interns may be considered for future full-time opportunities within the Agentis partner ecosystem.

Your Contribution

As a member of the principal investments team, you will be challenged from day one and will work closely with Partners, senior investment professionals, and management teams of portfolio companies. Your responsibilities will include:

 - Contributing to the operations of existing portfolio companies

 - Fulfilling corporate finance responsibilities that arise through the operations and

 - management of those portfolio companies

 - Researching and evaluating potential principal investment opportunities, including

 - analysis of target companies, sectors, industries, and geographies

 - Supporting the execution of principal investments, including financial analysis,

 - valuation, and transaction support

 - Building and maintaining financial models of varying complexity to support

 - investment decisions and portfolio monitoring

 - Supporting the development of investment theses, business plans, and strategic

 - initiatives for portfolio companies

 - Assisting with asset-level reporting and performance monitoring across the

 - principal investment portfolio

 - Preparing internal materials and presentations for investment committees and

 - senior leadership

As part of a high-performing, entrepreneurial team, work will be required in excess of a standard workday or workbook, and in line with typical hours for roles in capital markets.


Our Differentiators

 - Competitive compensation packages

 - Direct exposure to senior team members and industry leaders

 - Incredible opportunities for career growth including analyst track

 - Opportunities to work on projects with Agentis’ portfolio companies in diversified

 - sectors including consulting, healthcare, fintech, agriculture, and financial services

 - Learning and Development Opportunities

 - Hands-on learning through live principal investment work

 - Ongoing internal training in financial analysis, valuation, and investment decision-

 - making

 - Exposure to senior leadership and mentorship from experienced investors

Agentis acts as a sell-side and buy-side advisor on a wide variety of global transactions including renewable power, energy, digital, transportation, and public-private partnerships. Our clients include leading infrastructure funds, pension funds, contractors, and governments.

Agentis Capital Advisors is a business segment of Agentis Capital Partners, which operates a synergistic platform across four main business segments: Agentis Capital Partners (principal investments), Agentis Capital Advisors (financial advisory and asset management services), Agentis Capital Mining Partners (mining advisory), and Agentis Capital Markets (capital markets).


The Opportunity

As Vice President, Co-Investment Advisory, you will lead a team of dedicated professionals providing financial advisory services to limited and general partners seeking to deploy capital into co-investment opportunities, in a combination of infrastructure, debt, and other private-market assets.

You will be responsible for sourcing, developing and managing relationships with limited and general partners across Canada, the United States and globally.

You will play a significant role in the growth and direction of this business segment, seizing opportunities to expand into new geographies, verticals, and service offerings.

Your role will include:

 - Identifying, developing and managing relationships with limited and general partners active in infrastructure, and other co-investments

 - Identifying, originating and pitching co-investment advisory opportunities to limited partners, general partners and family offices

 - Pitching co-investment opportunities originated by other members of the team to limited partner and family office relationships

 - Preparing and presenting investment recommendations to client investment committees

 - Setting the strategic direction and implementing the strategic plan for the co-investment business unit

 - Directing the due diligence efforts of the team, including commercial, legal, tax, and financial

 - Liaising with the broader team for cross-group opportunities
   

Your Skills and Experience

 - Minimum six years of relevant experience in infrastructure, private equity, or private debt investing

 - Ability to develop and build strong client relationships

 - Strong communication and presentation skills, able to concisely communicate complex concepts

 - Demonstrated leadership experience through past professional roles

 - Established client relationships and network with limited partners, general partners and family offices is an asset

 - Ability to work effectively in a team environment
   

Position Type

 - Permanent Full-Time

 - Desired start date: Immediately

 - Location: Toronto

source_jobs.title: Investment Banking Associate - Public Private Partnership Transaction Advisory
source_jobs.description:
Our Firm

Agentis Capital Advisors is a leading global financial advisor with a reputation for delivering unparalleled value and advice to its clients. The firm has been the recipient of numerous recent industry rewards, including Financial Advisor of the Year in North America by P3 Awards (2024), Proximo (2022), and IJGlobal (2021).

The Opportunity

Our Associate program is designed to cultivate the quantitative and qualitative skills necessary to excel in an investment banking career. You will receive both formal and on-the-job training throughout your tenure. You will gain increasing levels of responsibility as you progress in your career, from overseeing the execution of financial model-related tasks, to managing multiple deal workstreams, to leading client relationships. You will be given unparalleled exposure and opportunities, including the chance to lead numerous transactions. As a result, our team members often achieve accelerated career growth and earn industry recognition. You will be recognized and rewarded for your individual performance within a close-knit team and meritocratic culture.



Your Contribution

As an integral member of the global public-private partnership (P3) advisory team, you will be challenged to accept increasing levels of responsibility as you develop your skills and reputation. Your work will directly impact our clients and the firm.

Your role will include:

 - Participating in the execution of PPP transactions

 - Developing complex financial models, including the use of macros and VBA programming

 - Assisting in the management of due diligence processes, including managing third-party advisors

 - Preparing client presentations and actively participating in discussions

 - Researching potential business development opportunities

 - Maintaining marketing materials for the firm, including creating pitches to be presented to potential clients

As a member of a high-performing team working in a fast-paced environment, there will be times where you will be required to work beyond a normal workday or workweek to fulfill the responsibilities of the role.



Our Differentiators

Breadth of experience and level of responsibility

 - Exceptional level of responsibility on complex transactions, allowing for rapid career progression

 - Opportunities to work on large global transactions

Training

 - Continuous internal training program

 - Internal leadership development program

Culture and approach

 - Entrepreneurial culture with a flat organization structure

 - Direct exposure to Partners and senior management across Agentis' offices

 - Defined path to achieving Partner, with all individuals entering the organization receiving the opportunity to become a full Partner in the business

Compensation and employee ownership

 - Uncapped performance-based bonus

 - Employee ownership program that commences for all individuals from the end of their first full fiscal-year of employment

Other benefits

 - Paid sabbatical every two years of employment

 - Comprehensive benefits including extended health care
   
   

Your Skills and Experience

 - A minimum of 2-3 years of experience in PPP transactions, including financial modeling

 - Ability to concisely communicate complex concepts and ideas verbally and in writing

 - Ability to take initiative and work independently with minimal supervision

 - Desire to assume increasing levels of responsibility and seek solutions to complex problems

 - Committed to producing accurate and thorough work product under tight deadlines

 - Ability to learn quickly and adapt in a fast-paced work environment

 - Resilient mindset in the face of adversity

 - Ability to work effectively in a team environment
   
   

Position Type

 - Permanent Full-Time

 - Desired start date: Immediately

 - Location: Vancouver or Toronto

source_jobs.title: Business Operations Consultant
source_jobs.description:
Our Firm

Agentis Capital Advisors is a leading global financial advisor with a reputation for delivering unparalleled value and advice to its clients. The firm has been the recipient of numerous industry rewards in recent years, including 2024 P3 Awards Financial Advisor of the Year, multiple 2023 industry deal awards, Proximo Infra's 2022 North American Financial Advisor of the Year, and IJGlobal's 2022 Judge's Choice Award. Our guiding principles and ability to generate value for clients sets us apart. Agentis acts as a sell-side and buy-side advisor on a wide variety of global transactions including renewable power, energy, digital, transportation, and public-private partnerships. Our clients include leading infrastructure funds, pension funds, contractors, and governments.

Agentis Capital Advisors is a business segment of Agentis Capital Partners, which operates a synergistic platform across four main business segments: Agentis Capital Partners (principal investments), Agentis Capital Advisors (financial advisory and asset management services), Agentis Capital Mining Partners (mining advisory), and Agentis Capital Markets (capital markets).

The Opportunity

The Business Operations Consultant will be responsible for collaborating with the Operations and senior leadership team to identify and execute strategies to help the company achieve its business objectives, improve employee performance, execute on improvement initiatives, and fulfill key operational activities. You will be working closely with the COO and the Operations Manager daily, and you will be an important member of a professional team and will be instrumental in shaping the culture and success of the firm. The role requires strong judgment, a high level of ownership, and the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment while communicating clearly and consistently with stakeholders. You will be an important member of a professional team, working with firm leadership and will be instrumental in shaping the culture and success of the firm.


The Business Operations Consultant will be responsible for, but not limited to, the following:

 - Designing, implementing, and monitoring operations projects, business processes, and IT systems to improve functional efficiency and effectiveness within the business operations

 - Supporting the development of our people and team effectiveness, including areas such as recruitment, training, performance management and workforce planning

 - Own and improve business processes across recruiting and onboarding, HR/people programs, learning and development, and the implementation of software solutions (e.g., CRM)

 - Developing and managing internal and external communications plans to drive operational effectiveness and to promote the business and company brand

 - Produce leadership reporting and analysis, including dashboards, presentations, and written updates on operational performance, risks, and progress against initiatives

 - Develop, manage, and apply AI and technology solutions to improve operational efficiency and effectiveness across core business processes.



Required Skills and Experience

 - Bachelor’s degree in a competitive field, preferably commerce, management, finance, or a related discipline, or equivalent practical experience

 - 1 to 3 years of experience in business operations, management consulting, internal strategy/operations, process improvement, recruiting operations, or a similar role

 - Proficiency with Microsoft Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, and Google Workspace is required; experience with Python, VBA, SQL, and recruitment software suites is an asset

 - Familiarity with full-cycle recruitment and understanding of HR practices is an asset

 - Project management experience is an asset

 - Knowledge of the Investment Banking and Financial Services Industry is an asset

 - Experience supporting business operations processes, including people operations and talent programs,

source_jobs.title: Managing Director - Digital Infrastructure, Investment Banking
source_jobs.description:
Our Firm

Agentis Capital Advisors is a leading global financial advisor with a reputation for delivering unparalleled value and strategic advice to its clients. Agentis acts as a sell-side and buy-side advisor with a focus on global transactions involving infrastructure, renewable power, energy, digital, transportation, and public-private partnerships.

With more than $125 billion in evaluated transactions and multiple industry awards, including North American Financial Advisor of the Year, we apply disciplined thinking, practical innovation, and an ownership mindset to every mandate. Our guiding principles and ability to generate value are our differentiators, which have earned the trust, respect, and business from leading infrastructure funds, pension funds, contractors, and governments.


Our Differentiators

 - Direct responsibility on complex, high-profile transactions across verticals and financial products

 - Direct involvement in SPAC and de-SPAC initiatives, with the opportunity to participate in associated economics

 - Competitive compensation with meaningful employee ownership and full benefits

 - Structured training and leadership development, including direct mentorship from Partners

 - Paid sabbatical every two years of employment

 - Entrepreneurial culture with a defined and achievable path to partnership

 - Co-investment rights alongside Partners on select principal opportunities
   

The Opportunity

Due to growth and an expanding remit across public-market acquisition vehicles, Agentis Capital Advisors is seeking a Managing Director, Investment Banking to lead and grow its digital infrastructure practice.

The Managing Director will play a pivotal role as part of the leadership team for Agentis’ North American investment banking practice, splitting time between (i) originating financial advisory mandates across digital infrastructure (and adjacent verticals), and (ii) leading and supporting SPAC and de-SPAC origination and execution across publicly listed acquisition vehicles. As a subject matter expert in digital infrastructure (including data centers, fiber, and towers), the Managing Director will also provide deal execution leadership and mentorship to junior bankers across the team.

The ideal candidate is an accomplished M&A banker with deep digital infrastructure sector expertise, a track record of sourcing and winning mandates, and a reputation for outstanding client service. An established network of existing or prospective clients in North America is expected.



Key Responsibilities

Deal Origination & Revenue Generation:

 - Identify and pursue new M&A advisory opportunities in digital infrastructure (such as data centers, fiber, and towers) and adjacent energy verticals, aligned with Agentis’ platform and growth strategy

 - Lead and support SPAC target origination and de-SPAC execution, screening private and public companies against SPAC-over-alternatives criteria

 - Lead pitch presentations to win mandates with the support of a talented, experienced team

 - Meet individual and firm revenue targets through successful deal closures, including SPAC and de-SPAC mandates

Senior Client Relationship Management:

 - Serve as the primary point of contact for investors, C-suite executives, board members, and government officials.

 - Build long-term partnerships with institutional investors such as pension funds, private wealth offices, and infrastructure private equity firms.

Strategic Advisory:

 - Provide senior counsel on M&A, capital raising, and complex financing structures across digital infrastructure and adjacent verticals

 - Originate and support financial advisory mandates as required, drawing on sector expertise and target-screening capability across private and public-market opportunities

 - Advise clients on the impact of market dynamics, industry trends, and regulatory developments

Leadership & Team Mentorship:

 - Lead, motivate, and mentor deal teams to meet execution delive

source_jobs.title: Investment Banking Summer Analyst - Infrastructure M&A Advisory - Summer 2027
source_jobs.description:
Our Firm

Agentis Capital Advisors is a leading global financial advisor with a reputation for delivering unparalleled value and advice to its clients. The firm has been the recipient of numerous industry rewards in recent years, including 2024 P3 Awards Financial Advisor of the Year. Our clients include leading infrastructure funds, pension funds, contractors, and governments.

The Opportunity

The intern program includes a formal program of instruction in project finance, financial statement analysis, and financial modeling. Analyst interns are expected to demonstrate financial modeling capabilities through real-world applications and will be invited to work on active projects. You will be recognized and rewarded for your individual performance within a close-knit team and meritocratic culture. Upon successful completion of the internship, interns have the opportunity to be invited back to join Agentis in a full-time position.

Your Contribution

As an analyst intern, you can expect to be challenged from day one by complex, high-pressure assignments that will directly impact our clients and the firm. As a member of our advisory team, you will work closely in groups led by senior partners and will have exposure to a wide variety of transactions within a performance-based culture.

Your role will include:

 - Participating in the execution of equity and debt offerings, mergers and acquisitions, public-private partnerships, and principal investments

 - Assisting in the development of complex financial models for infrastructure transactions, including the use of macros and VBA programming

 - Assisting in the management of due diligence processes, including managing third-party advisors

 - Developing asset valuations using a variety of approaches

 - Preparing client presentations and participating in discussions

 - Assisting the senior team in the evaluation of investment opportunities and management of existing investments

 - Researching potential investment banking opportunities; providing research on target companies, sectors, industries, and geographies

 - Maintaining marketing materials for the firm, including creating pitch decks to be presented to potential clients.

 - As a member of a high-performing team working in a fast-paced environment, there will be times where you will be required to work beyond a normal workday or workweek to fulfill the responsibilities of the role.

Our Differentiators

 - Competitive compensation packages

 - Direct exposure to senior team members and industry leaders

 - Incredible opportunities for career growth including analyst track

 - Opportunities to work on projects with one of Agentis’ portfolio companies in other sectors including consulting, healthcare, fintech, and agriculture

 - Participation in Agentis’ internal training modules including a leadership development program

 - Regular team events

 - Potential to be invited back for a full-time position

Your Skills and Experience

 - An undergraduate degree in accounting, finance, commerce, business, statistics, computer science, engineering, mathematics, or other related areas

 - Entering your penultimate year of study (i.e. graduation by summer 2028)

 - Exceptional academic results (minimum cGPA of 80% or 3.7/4.0)

 - Ability to concisely communicate complex concepts and ideas verbally and

source_jobs.title: Vice President, Investment Banking - Renewables
Analyst - Principal Investments
source_jobs.description:
OUR FIRM

Agentis Capital Partners ("ACP") operates as the family office of the founding partners of Agentis, a diversified platform with businesses spanning principal investments, financial advisory, capital markets, and asset management services. Since its founding in 2009, the Agentis platform has advised on more than $30bn of transactions across infrastructure, mining, and energy, while ACP has built and managed a long-standing portfolio of principal investments across technology, industrial, and mixed industrial-technology businesses.

ACP actively pursues both active and passive principal investments and currently manages a portfolio of more than 20 businesses. The firm has consistently made principal investments over the past decade, recycling capital into high-value opportunities and working closely with management teams and boards to create long-term value.



YOUR CONTRIBUTION

As an integral member of the principal investments team, you will work directly with the founding partners of Agentis on principal activities and will work with members of the advisory team where Agentis is making an investment in or alongside a client. You will work directly with C-suite executives and board members of Agentis portfolio companies and potential investments.

Your role will include:

 - Researching potential investment opportunities; providing research on target companies, sectors, industries, and geographies

 - Evaluating investment opportunities and participating in the execution of principal investments
   
   - Developing financial models with a range of complexity for principal transactions
   
   - Assisting in the management of due diligence processes, including managing third-party advisors

 - Assisting in developing the strategy, business plan, and objectives of portfolio investments

 - Managing and aggregating asset-level reporting for portfolio investments

As a member of a high-performing team working in a fast-paced environment, you will be required to work beyond a normal workday or workweek to fulfill your responsibilities.



OUR DIFFERENTIATORS

 - Breadth of experience and level of responsibility
   
   - Exceptional level of responsibility on complex transactions and assets
   
   - Direct engagement with founding partners, C-suite executives of portfolio investments, and board members of portfolio investments
   
   - Wide breadth of work across industries

 - Training
   
   - Continuous internal training program
   
   - Internal leadership development program

 - Culture and approach
   
   - Entrepreneurial culture with a flat organization structure
   
   - Defined path to achieving Partner, with all individuals joining a Partner-track role receiving the opportunity to become a full Partner in the business

 - Compensation and employee ownership
   
   - Exceptional compensation versus the market for those who deliver exceptional performance, with uncapped performance-based bonuses
   
   - Employee ownership program that commences for all individuals from the end of their first full fiscal year of employment

 - Other benefits
   
   - Immediate access to principal investment opportunities alongside Partners
   
   - Paid sabbatical every two years of employment
   
   - Ability to work temporarily out of our Vancouver or Toronto office
     


YOUR BACKGROUND AND SKILLS

 - An undergraduate degree in accounting, finance, commerce, business, statistics, computer science or other related fields

 - Exceptional academic results (minimum cGPA of 80% or 3.7/4.0)

 - Ability to concisely communicate complex concepts and ideas verbally and in writing

 - Ability to take initiative and work independently with minimal supervision

 - Desire to assume increasing levels of responsibility and seek solutions to complex problems

 - Committed to producing accurate and thorough work product under tight deadlines

 - Ability to learn quickly and adapt in a fast-paced work environment

 - Resilient mindset in the f

The Opportunity

As Vice President, Co-Investment Advisory, you will lead a team of dedicated professionals providing financial advisory services to limited and general partners seeking to deploy capital into co-investment opportunities, in a combination of infrastructure, debt, and other private-market assets.

You will be responsible for sourcing, developing and managing relationships with limited and general partners across Canada, the United States and globally.

You will play a significant role in the growth and direction of this business segment, seizing opportunities to expand into new geographies, verticals, and service offerings.

Your role will include:

 - Identifying, developing and managing relationships with limited and general partners active in infrastructure, and other co-investments

 - Identifying, originating and pitching co-investment advisory opportunities to limited partners, general partners and family offices

 - Pitching co-investment opportunities originated by other members of the team to limited partner and family office relationships

 - Preparing and presenting investment recommendations to client investment committees

 - Setting the strategic direction and implementing the strategic plan for the co-investment business unit

 - Directing the due diligence efforts of the team, including commercial, legal, tax, and financial

 - Liaising with the broader team for cross-group opportunities
   

Your Skills and Experience

 - Minimum six years of relevant experience in infrastructure, private equity, or private debt investing

 - Ability to develop and build strong client relationships

 - Strong communication and presentation skills, able to concisely communicate complex concepts

 - Demonstrated leadership experience through past professional roles

 - Established client relationships and network with limited partners, general partners and family offices is an asset

 - Ability to work effectively in a team environment
   

Position Type

 - Permanent Full-Time

 - Desired start date: Immediately

 - Location: Remote

source_jobs.title: Managing Director, Investment Banking - Infrastructure M&A Advisory
source_jobs.description:
Our Firm

Agentis Capital Advisors is a leading global financial advisor with a reputation for delivering unparalleled value and strategic advice to its clients. Agentis acts as a sell-side and buy-side advisor with a focus on global transactions involving infrastructure, renewable power, energy, digital, transportation, and public-private partnerships. With more than $125 billion in evaluated transactions and multiple industry awards, including North American Financial Advisor of the Year, we apply disciplined thinking, practical innovation, and an ownership mindset to every mandate. Our guiding principles and ability to generate value are our differentiators, which have earned the trust, respect, and business from leading infrastructure funds, pension funds, contractors, and governments.


Our Differentiators

 - Direct responsibility on complex, high-profile transactions across verticals and financial products

 - Global mandate flow from a North American base

 - Structured training and leadership development, including direct mentorship from Partners

 - Competitive compensation with meaningful employee ownership and full benefits

 - Paid sabbatical every two years of employment

 - Entrepreneurial culture with a defined and achievable path to partnership

 - Co-investment rights alongside Partners on select principal opportunities

The Opportunity

Due to growth, Agentis Capital Advisors is seeking a Managing Director.

 

The Managing Director will play a pivotal role as part of the leadership team for Agentis’ North American financial advisory practice, driving growth through senior client relationships, business origination, and revenue generation. The MD will also provide deal execution leadership and mentorship to junior bankers across the team.

 

The ideal candidate is an accomplished sell-side or buy-side banker with a track record of sourcing and winning mandates, deep sector expertise, and a reputation for outstanding client service.

 

Key Responsibilities

Deal Origination & Revenue Generation:

 - Identify and pursue new business opportunities aligned with Agentis’ platform and growth strategy

 - Lead pitch presentations to win mandates with the support of a talented, experienced team.

 - Meet individual and firm revenue targets through successful deal closures

Senior Client Relationship Management:

 - Serve as the primary point of contact for investors, C-suite executives, board members, and government officials.

 - Build long-term partnerships with institutional investors such as pension funds, private wealth offices, and infrastructure private equity firms.

Strategic Advisory:

 - Provide senior counsel on M&A, capital raising, and complex financing structures across infrastructure verticals

 - Advise clients on the impact of market dynamics, industry trends, and regulatory developments

Leadership & Team Mentorship:

 - Lead, motivate, and mentor deal teams to meet execution deliverables, staffing commitments, and performance objectives

 - Assist in the recruitment and development of top talent to maintain a competitive practice

Deal Execution Oversight:

 - Oversee deal execution end-to-end, ensuring work product, timelines, and commercial terms align with client expectations and firm standards

Skills and Experience

 - Minimum ten years of infrastructure investment banking or financial advisory experience, with a proven track record in origination, business development, and client relationship management

 - Demonstrated ability to structure, execute, and close infrastructure investment mandates

 - Strong deal management and technical delivery capabilities

 - Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills

 - Ability to work under pressure and consistently deliver high-quality work with strict deadlines to meet team and client objectives (often while being geographically dispersed)

 - Willingness and ability to travel domestically and internationally as required

 - While all invest

notes: Financial services firm with no tech roles
